creator: ports@nicandneal.net
text:
Hi. Thanks for your effort.

Things are like they are for a reason, which I think you have overlooked. Nimble is not "included" in the build. It's in a separate repository and is git cloned during the build, as is also now atlas, it's potential replacement. I believe that this is forbidden for ports as the sources are not under ports control and thus we just don't know what we're getting.

I am in the process of updating the port, but have still (at least) one problem left. There are significant changes in this update, even though it's in theory a simple evolution. The current port places things in the correct places in the directory structure, but this may have to change as it's becoming too difficult to bludgeon the nim system into using them. I may have to change the structure to use a /usr/local/nim directory if I can't fix the current problem (which is being unable to reference pure libraries). Time will tell.

Unfortunately I am unable to work on this for the next week or so, so the upgrading of the nim port will have to wait a bit.
